title: Leaders Eat Last: Why Some Teams Pull Together and Others Don't
description: Simon Sinek draws on biology, anthropology, and military history to explain why great leaders create environments of trust and cooperation. Rooted in the biology of the limbic system, the book shows how leaders who put their people's safety before their own unlock the extraordinary potential of every team member.
keyConcepts: Four chemicals: Dopamine, Serotonin, Endorphin, Oxytocin
ABCDs of Leadership: Abundance / Belonging / Connectedness / Direction
Self-sacrifice as the hallmark of true leaders
Pharmaceutical rewards substituting for human connection
Leader as the architect of culture
notableQuotes: When leaders create a Circle of Safety, they reduce the threats people feel inside the group, which frees them up to focus more time and energy to protect the organization from the constant dangers outside and seize the big opportunities.
The responsibility of a leader is not to do all the work yourself. It is to create a work environment in which everyone wants to contribute their best.
Leadership is a choice, not a rank.
If your actions inspire others to dream more, learn more, do more and become more, you are a leader.
The main challenge companies face today is human: how to make people feel like they belong to a team and care about the work they do.
